activation_results.reason: Input is a verbatim match for the skill's canonical use case.
Swapping Audit for Review preserves intent and platform list.
User explicitly invokes the skill by name.
SSH key forwarding is unrelated to CI/CD pipeline auditing.
Asking what skills are is meta and out of scope.

criterion_results.text: No American spellings present in the narrative (color, optimize, behavior, organize, center, defense, license as a verb, analyze) — Australian or neutral forms both pass
criterion_results.evidence: Uses 'categorised', 'artefact'; none of the banned American spellings appear.

criterion_results.text: Findings cite file:line evidence and are categorised by severity
criterion_results.evidence: Risk Register and per-workflow tables list e.g. release.yml:17, deploy-prod.yml:33 with CRITICAL/HIGH/MEDIUM severity columns.

criterion_results.text: Report includes the canonical sections: 'Executive Summary', 'Category Scores'
criterion_results.evidence: Both '## Executive Summary' and '## Category Scores' headings appear in the artefact.

criterion_results.text: Artefact references the input path or target by name (no placeholder paths)
criterion_results.evidence: Names target 'acme-api' and concrete workflow files such as .github/workflows/release.yml and deploy-prod.yml.

judge_result.notes: Artefact satisfies all four criteria — narrative is Australian/neutral, findings carry file:line evidence with explicit severity, both canonical headings (Executive Summary, Category Scores) are present, and concrete target plus workflow paths are referenced rather than placeholders.
